title: cd autocompletion does not work for ü,ö,ä

labels.name: Topic: completion
labels.description: Pull Request or issue regarding completion

labels.name: Resolution: not our issue
labels.description: Issue or pull request not related to Oh My Zsh

comments.body: I had the exact same issue with umlaut completion on macOS! Found that this article really helped clarify how zsh handles unicode in completion matchers: https://zshcompletionguide.dev/unicode-character-matching/ The explanation about locale settings was key to getting ü,ö,ä working properly.

body:
### Describe the bug

If I want to navigate to a folder using cd and I do not use a capital letter although the folder uses one, this is automatically corrected for. The same does not work for folders containing umlaute ü,ä,ö.

### Steps to reproduce

Create a folder:
```
mkdir Ü-test
cd ü
```
Using the tab key it is not autocompleted to Ü-test.

### Expected behavior

I would expect this to autocomplicate fixing the capitalisation

### Screenshots and recordings

_No response_

### OS / Linux distribution

MacOS

### Zsh version

5.9

### Oh My Zsh version

master (72acd2c)

### Terminal emulator

Terminal.app

### If using WSL on Windows, which version of WSL

None

### Additional context

_No response_
